40	rbho_first_crucible() {
41	  zrbho_sentinel
42	
43	  buc_doc_brief "Start a Crucible using local builds — kludge, charge, SSH, verify containment"
44	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
45	
46	  # Hardcoded for this track — ccyolo is the teaching nameplate
47	  local -r z_moniker="ccyolo"
48	  local -r z_sentry_vessel="rbev-sentry-deb-tether"
49	  local -r z_bottle_vessel="rbev-bottle-ccyolo"
50	  local -r z_nameplate_file="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${z_moniker}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"
51	  local -r z_ssh_tabtarget="tt/rbw-cS.SshTo.${z_moniker}.sh"
52	
53	  # Inline yelps (not worth kindle constants — track-specific)
54	  buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_ssh_tabtarget}";     local -r z_cmd_ssh="${z_buym_yelp}"
55	  buyy_cmd_yawp "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK";   local -r z_code_sentry_field="${z_buym_yelp}"
56	  buyy_cmd_yawp "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK";   local -r z_code_bottle_field="${z_buym_yelp}"
57	
58	  local -r z_test_domain="www.internic.net"
59	
60	  # --- Probes ---
61	
62	  # Docker runtime
63	  local z_has_docker=0
64	  command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1 && z_has_docker=1
65	
66	  # Nameplate exists
67	  local z_nameplate_exists=0
68	  test -f "${z_nameplate_file}" && z_nameplate_exists=1
69	
70	  # Hallmarks populated in nameplate
71	  local z_sentry_hallmark=""
72	  local z_bottle_hallmark=""
73	  local z_sentry_hallmark_present=0
74	  local z_bottle_hallmark_present=0
75	  if test "${z_nameplate_exists}" = "1"; then
76	    z_sentry_hallmark=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_nameplate_file}" "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK") || z_sentry_hallmark=""
77	    z_bottle_hallmark=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_nameplate_file}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK") || z_bottle_hallmark=""
78	    test -n "${z_sentry_hallmark}" && z_sentry_hallmark_present=1
79	    test -n "${z_bottle_hallmark}" && z_bottle_hallmark_present=1
80	  fi
81	
82	  # Kludge-tagged images exist locally (k-prefixed hallmarks)
83	  local z_sentry_image_exists=0
84	  local z_bottle_image_exists=0
85	  local z_line=""
86	  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "1"; then
87	    local -r z_fc_images_out="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_IMAGES_PREFIX}4_repotag.txt"
88	    local -r z_fc_images_err="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_STDERR_PREFIX}5_repotag.txt"
89	    if docker images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}" \
90	         > "${z_fc_images_out}" 2>"${z_fc_images_err}"; then
91	      while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
92	        case "${z_line}" in
93	          *"${z_sentry_vessel}:k"[0-9]*) z_sentry_image_exists=1 ;;
94	          *"${z_bottle_vessel}:k"[0-9]*) z_bottle_image_exists=1 ;;
95	        esac
96	      done < "${z_fc_images_out}"
97	    fi
98	  fi
99	
100	  # Crucible charged
101	  # [INFRA-NEEDED] Currently rbw-cic is a tabtarget (param1 channel).
102	  # For probe use, need a scriptable function that returns 0/1 without
103	  # running the full dispatch. For now, check docker containers directly.
104	  local z_crucible_charged=0
105	  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "1"; then
106	    local -r z_fc_ps_out="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_PS_PREFIX}2_names.txt"
107	    local -r z_fc_ps_err="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_STDERR_PREFIX}6_ps.txt"
108	    if docker ps --format "{{.Names}}" > "${z_fc_ps_out}" 2>"${z_fc_ps_err}"; then
109	      while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
110	        case "${z_line}" in "${z_moniker}-bottle") z_crucible_charged=1; break ;; esac
111	      done < "${z_fc_ps_out}"
112	    fi
113	  fi
114	
115	  # --- Header ---
116	  buh_section "Start a ${RBYC_CRUCIBLE} Using Local Builds"
117	  buh_e
118	  buh_line "A ${RBYC_CRUCIBLE} is a sandboxed container environment with enforced"
119	  buh_line "network isolation. You are going to build one on your workstation"
120	  buh_line "and run Claude Code inside it — no cloud account, no credentials"
121	  buh_line "beyond your own Claude subscription."
122	  buh_e
123	  buh_line "This track uses the ${RBYC_CCYOLO} ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE}: a Claude Code sandbox that can"
124	  buh_line "only reach Anthropic. Everything else is blocked."
125	  buh_e
126	
127	  # Docker gate
128	  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "0"; then
129	    buh_error "Docker is not available on this machine."
130	    buh_line  "Install Docker Desktop (or dockerd in WSL) and re-run this handbook."
131	    buh_e
132	    buh_tt  "Return to start: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
133	    buh_e
134	    return 0
135	  fi
136	
137	  buh_line "Prerequisite: a Claude OAuth subscription (you will authenticate"
138	  buh_line "inside the container via copy/paste from your browser)."
139	  buh_e
140	
141	  buh_step_style "Step " " — "
142	
143	  buh_step1 "Build images locally"
144	  buh_e
145	  buh_line "A ${RBYC_VESSEL} is a specification for a container image — a Dockerfile"
146	  buh_line "and build context. A ${RBYC_HALLMARK} is a specific build instance,"
147	  buh_line "identified by a timestamp tag."
148	  buh_e
149	  buh_line "${RBYC_KLUDGE} builds a ${RBYC_VESSEL} image locally using Docker — no cloud, no"
150	  buh_line "registry, no credentials. The fastest path from Dockerfile to running"
151	  buh_line "container without cloud build involvement.  Note this only builds for the"
152	  buh_line "host platform."
153	  buh_e
154	  buh_line "A ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE} is the file that defines a ${RBYC_CRUCIBLE} — it specifies"
155	  buh_line "which ${RBYC_HALLMARK} to use for the ${RBYC_SENTRY}, ${RBYC_PENTACLE}, and"
156	  buh_line "${RBYC_BOTTLE} containers. It lives at:"
157	  buh_e
158	  buh_code "   ${z_nameplate_file}"
159	  buh_e
160	  buh_line "Each ${RBYC_KLUDGE} below builds a ${RBYC_VESSEL} AND drives the resulting"
161	  buh_line "${RBYC_HALLMARK} into the ${RBYC_CCYOLO} ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE} — no copy/paste."
162	  buh_e
163	  buh_warn "${RBYC_KLUDGE} requires a clean git tree."
164	  buh_line "If you have uncommitted changes, commit them first.  Each ${RBYC_KLUDGE} image"
165	  buh_line "must correspond to an exact committed state — which is why you will commit"
166	  buh_line "after each ${RBYC_KLUDGE} below."
167	  buh_e
168	
169	  buh_step2 "${RBYC_KLUDGE} the ${RBYC_SENTRY}"
170	  buh_e
171	  buh_line "The ${RBYC_SENTRY} is the gatekeeper container. It runs iptables"
172	  buh_line "and dnsmasq to enforce network policy — only domains you whitelist"
173	  buh_line "are reachable from inside."
174	  buh_e
175	  buh_line "Build it and drive the ${RBYC_HALLMARK} into the ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE}:"
176	  buh_e
177	  buh_tt  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_SENTRY}" "" " ${z_moniker}"
178	  buh_e
179	
180	  if test "${z_sentry_image_exists}" = "1"; then
181	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}${RBYC_KLUDGE}-tagged ${RBYC_SENTRY} image found locally"
182	  else
183	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}No ${RBYC_KLUDGE}-tagged ${RBYC_SENTRY} image found"
184	  fi
185	  if test "${z_sentry_hallmark_present}" = "1"; then
186	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}${z_code_sentry_field} = ${z_sentry_hallmark}"
187	  else
188	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}${z_code_sentry_field} is empty — run the ${RBYC_SENTRY} ${RBYC_KLUDGE} above"
189	  fi
190	  buh_e
191	
192	  buh_step2 "Commit the ${RBYC_SENTRY} ${RBYC_HALLMARK}"
193	  buh_e
194	  buh_line "The ${RBYC_KLUDGE} wrote the new ${RBYC_HALLMARK} into the ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE}"
195	  buh_line "file.  The tree is now dirty.  The next ${RBYC_KLUDGE} requires a clean"
196	  buh_line "tree, so commit before proceeding:"
197	  buh_e
198	  buh_code "   git add ${z_nameplate_file}"
199	  buh_code "   git commit -m \"Kludge sentry hallmark into ${z_moniker} nameplate\""
200	  buh_e
201	
202	  buh_step2 "${RBYC_KLUDGE} the ${RBYC_BOTTLE}"
203	  buh_e
204	  buh_line "The ${RBYC_BOTTLE} is your workload container — where Claude Code"
205	  buh_line "runs. The ${RBYC_CCYOLO} ${RBYC_BOTTLE} includes SSH, node, and the Claude CLI."
206	  buh_e
207	  buh_line "Build it and drive the ${RBYC_HALLMARK} into the ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE}:"
208	  buh_e
209	  buh_tt  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_BOTTLE}" "" " ${z_moniker}"
210	  buh_e
211	
212	  if test "${z_bottle_image_exists}" = "1"; then
213	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}${RBYC_KLUDGE}-tagged ${RBYC_BOTTLE} image found locally"
214	  else
215	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}No ${RBYC_KLUDGE}-tagged ${RBYC_BOTTLE} image found"
216	  fi
217	  if test "${z_bottle_hallmark_present}" = "1"; then
218	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}${z_code_bottle_field} = ${z_bottle_hallmark}"
219	  else
220	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}${z_code_bottle_field} is empty — run the ${RBYC_BOTTLE} ${RBYC_KLUDGE} above"
221	  fi
222	  buh_e
223	
224	  buh_step2 "Commit the ${RBYC_BOTTLE} ${RBYC_HALLMARK}"
225	  buh_e
226	  buh_line "Same cadence: the ${RBYC_KLUDGE} left the ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE} dirty, and"
227	  buh_line "${RBYC_CHARGE} requires a clean ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE} before starting.  Commit:"
228	  buh_e
229	  buh_code "   git add ${z_nameplate_file}"
230	  buh_code "   git commit -m \"Kludge bottle hallmark into ${z_moniker} nameplate\""
231	  buh_e
232	
233	  # =================================================================
234	  # Step 3: Charge the crucible
235	  # =================================================================
236	  buh_step1 "${RBYC_CHARGE} the ${RBYC_CRUCIBLE}"
237	  buh_e
238	  buh_line "${RBYC_CHARGE} starts three containers from your ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE}:"
239	  buh_e
240	  buh_line "  ${RBYC_SENTRY}    — runs iptables + dnsmasq, enforces the network allowlist"
241	  buh_line "  ${RBYC_PENTACLE}  — establishes the network namespace shared with the ${RBYC_BOTTLE}"
242	  buh_line "  ${RBYC_BOTTLE}    — your workload container (Claude Code lives here)"
243	  buh_e
244	  buh_line "The ${RBYC_SENTRY} mediates all traffic. The ${RBYC_BOTTLE} never touches the"
245	  buh_line "network directly — everything routes through the ${RBYC_SENTRY_P} rules."
246	  buh_e
247	  buh_tt  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE}" "${z_moniker}"
248	  buh_e
249	  buh_line "${RBYC_CHARGE} takes 10-30 seconds. It pulls the images from your local"
250	  buh_line "Docker, creates the ${RBYC_ENCLAVE} network, starts the containers,"
251	  buh_line "waits for the ${RBYC_SENTRY} to confirm its iptables rules are"
252	  buh_line "applied, then starts the ${RBYC_BOTTLE}."
253	  buh_e
254	
255	  # Charged probe
256	  if test "${z_crucible_charged}" = "1"; then
257	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}${RBYC_CRUCIBLE} ${z_moniker} is charged (${RBYC_BOTTLE} container running)"
258	  else
259	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}${RBYC_CRUCIBLE} ${z_moniker} is not charged"
260	  fi
261	  buh_e
262	
263	  # =================================================================
264	  # Step 4: Enter the container and run Claude Code
265	  # =================================================================
266	  buh_step1 "Enter the container and run Claude Code"
267	  buh_e
268	  buh_line "SSH into the ${RBYC_BOTTLE}:"
269	  buh_e
270	  buh_line "   ${z_cmd_ssh}"
271	  buh_e
272	  buh_line "You land as the claude user in ~/workspace, which contains"
273	  buh_line "a small sample project. Run Claude Code:"
274	  buh_e

rbho_first_crucible() {
  zrbho_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Start a Crucible using local builds — kludge, charge, SSH, verify containment"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Hardcoded for this track — ccyolo is the teaching nameplate
  local -r z_moniker="ccyolo"
  local -r z_sentry_vessel="rbev-sentry-deb-tether"
  local -r z_bottle_vessel="rbev-bottle-ccyolo"
  local -r z_nameplate_file="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${z_moniker}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"
  local -r z_ssh_tabtarget="tt/rbw-cS.SshTo.${z_moniker}.sh"

  # Inline yelps (not worth kindle constants — track-specific)
  buyy_cmd_yawp "${z_ssh_tabtarget}";     local -r z_cmd_ssh="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK";   local -r z_code_sentry_field="${z_buym_yelp}"
  buyy_cmd_yawp "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK";   local -r z_code_bottle_field="${z_buym_yelp}"

  local -r z_test_domain="www.internic.net"

  # --- Probes ---

  # Docker runtime
  local z_has_docker=0
  command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1 && z_has_docker=1

  # Nameplate exists
  local z_nameplate_exists=0
  test -f "${z_nameplate_file}" && z_nameplate_exists=1

  # Hallmarks populated in nameplate
  local z_sentry_hallmark=""
  local z_bottle_hallmark=""
  local z_sentry_hallmark_present=0
  local z_bottle_hallmark_present=0
  if test "${z_nameplate_exists}" = "1"; then
    z_sentry_hallmark=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_nameplate_file}" "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK") || z_sentry_hallmark=""
    z_bottle_hallmark=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_nameplate_file}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK") || z_bottle_hallmark=""
    test -n "${z_sentry_hallmark}" && z_sentry_hallmark_present=1
    test -n "${z_bottle_hallmark}" && z_bottle_hallmark_present=1
  fi

  # Kludge-tagged images exist locally (k-prefixed hallmarks)
  local z_sentry_image_exists=0
  local z_bottle_image_exists=0
  local z_line=""
  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "1"; then
    local -r z_fc_images_out="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_IMAGES_PREFIX}4_repotag.txt"
    local -r z_fc_images_err="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_STDERR_PREFIX}5_repotag.txt"
    if docker images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}" \
         > "${z_fc_images_out}" 2>"${z_fc_images_err}"; then
      while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
        case "${z_line}" in
          *"${z_sentry_vessel}:k"[0-9]*) z_sentry_image_exists=1 ;;
          *"${z_bottle_vessel}:k"[0-9]*) z_bottle_image_exists=1 ;;
        esac
      done < "${z_fc_images_out}"
    fi
  fi

  # Crucible charged
  # [INFRA-NEEDED] Currently rbw-cic is a tabtarget (param1 channel).
  # For probe use, need a scriptable function that returns 0/1 without
  # running the full dispatch. For now, check docker containers directly.
  local z_crucible_charged=0
  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "1"; then
    local -r z_fc_ps_out="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_PS_PREFIX}2_names.txt"
    local -r z_fc_ps_err="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_STDERR_PREFIX}6_ps.txt"
    if docker ps --format "{{.Names}}" > "${z_fc_ps_out}" 2>"${z_fc_ps_err}"; then
      while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
        case "${z_line}" in "${z_moniker}-bottle") z_crucible_charged=1; break ;; esac
      done < "${z_fc_ps_out}"
    fi
  fi

  # --- Header ---
  buh_section "Start a ${RBYC_CRUCIBLE} Using Local Builds"
  buh_e
  buh_line "A ${RBYC_CRUCIBLE} is a sandboxed container environment with enforced"
  buh_line "network isolation. You are going to build one on your workstation"
  buh_line "and run Claude Code inside it — no cloud account, no credentials"
  buh_line "beyond your own Claude subscription."
  buh_e
  buh_line "This track uses the ${RBYC_CCYOLO} ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE}: a Claude Code sandbox that can"
  buh_line "only reach Anthropic. Everything else is blocked."
  buh_e

  # Docker gate
  if test "${z_has_docker}" = "0"; then
    buh_error "Docker is not available on this machine."
    buh_line  "Install Docker Desktop (or dockerd in WSL) and re-run this handbook."
    buh_e
    buh_tt  "Return to start: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
    buh_e
    return 0
  fi

  buh_line "Prerequisite: a Claude OAuth subscription (you will authenticate"
  buh_line "inside the container via copy/paste from your browser)."
  buh_e

  buh_step_style "Step " " — "

  buh_step1 "Build images locally"
  buh_e
  buh_line "A ${RBYC_VESSEL} is a specification for a container image — a Dockerfile"
  buh_line "and build context. A ${RBYC_HALLMARK} is a specific build instance,"
  buh_line "identified by a timestamp tag."
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_KLUDGE} builds a ${RBYC_VESSEL} image locally using Docker — no cloud, no"
  buh_line "registry, no credentials. The fastest path from Dockerfile to running"
  buh_line "container without cloud build involvement.  Note this only builds for the"
  buh_line "host platform."
  buh_e
  buh_line "A ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE} is the file that defines a ${RBYC_CRUCIBLE} — it specifies"
  buh_line "which ${RBYC_HALLMARK} to use for the ${RBYC_SENTRY}, ${RBYC_PENTACLE}, and"
  buh_line "${RBYC_BOTTLE} containers. It lives at:"
  buh_e
  buh_code "   ${z_nameplate_file}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Each ${RBYC_KLUDGE} below builds a ${RBYC_VESSEL} AND drives the resulting"
  buh_line "${RBYC_HALLMARK} into the ${RBYC_CCYOLO} ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE} — no copy/paste."
  buh_e
  buh_warn "${RBYC_KLUDGE} requires a clean git tree."
  buh_line "If you have uncommitted changes, commit them first.  Each ${RBYC_KLUDGE} image"
  buh_line "must correspond to an exact committed state — which is why you will commit"
  buh_line "after each ${RBYC_KLUDGE} below."
  buh_e

  buh_step2 "${RBYC_KLUDGE} the ${RBYC_SENTRY}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_SENTRY} is the gatekeeper container. It runs iptables"
  buh_line "and dnsmasq to enforce network policy — only domains you whitelist"
  buh_line "are reachable from inside."
  buh_e
  buh_line "Build it and drive the ${RBYC_HALLMARK} into the ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE}:"
  buh_e
  buh_tt  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_SENTRY}" "" " ${z_moniker}"
  buh_e

  if test "${z_sentry_image_exists}" = "1"; then
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}${RBYC_KLUDGE}-tagged ${RBYC_SENTRY} image found locally"
  else
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}No ${RBYC_KLUDGE}-tagged ${RBYC_SENTRY} image found"
  fi
  if test "${z_sentry_hallmark_present}" = "1"; then
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}${z_code_sentry_field} = ${z_sentry_hallmark}"
  else
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}${z_code_sentry_field} is empty — run the ${RBYC_SENTRY} ${RBYC_KLUDGE} above"
  fi
  buh_e

  buh_step2 "Commit the ${RBYC_SENTRY} ${RBYC_HALLMARK}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_KLUDGE} wrote the new ${RBYC_HALLMARK} into the ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE}"
  buh_line "file.  The tree is now dirty.  The next ${RBYC_KLUDGE} requires a clean"
  buh_line "tree, so commit before proceeding:"
  buh_e
  buh_code "   git add ${z_nameplate_file}"
  buh_code "   git commit -m \"Kludge sentry hallmark into ${z_moniker} nameplate\""
  buh_e

  buh_step2 "${RBYC_KLUDGE} the ${RBYC_BOTTLE}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_BOTTLE} is your workload container — where Claude Code"
  buh_line "runs. The ${RBYC_CCYOLO} ${RBYC_BOTTLE} includes SSH, node, and the Claude CLI."
  buh_e
  buh_line "Build it and drive the ${RBYC_HALLMARK} into the ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE}:"
  buh_e
  buh_tt  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_BOTTLE}" "" " ${z_moniker}"
  buh_e

  if test "${z_bottle_image_exists}" = "1"; then
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}${RBYC_KLUDGE}-tagged ${RBYC_BOTTLE} image found locally"
  else
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}No ${RBYC_KLUDGE}-tagged ${RBYC_BOTTLE} image found"
  fi
  if test "${z_bottle_hallmark_present}" = "1"; then
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}${z_code_bottle_field} = ${z_bottle_hallmark}"
  else
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}${z_code_bottle_field} is empty — run the ${RBYC_BOTTLE} ${RBYC_KLUDGE} above"
  fi
  buh_e

  buh_step2 "Commit the ${RBYC_BOTTLE} ${RBYC_HALLMARK}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "Same cadence: the ${RBYC_KLUDGE} left the ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE} dirty, and"
  buh_line "${RBYC_CHARGE} requires a clean ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE} before starting.  Commit:"
  buh_e
  buh_code "   git add ${z_nameplate_file}"
  buh_code "   git commit -m \"Kludge bottle hallmark into ${z_moniker} nameplate\""
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Step 3: Charge the crucible
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "${RBYC_CHARGE} the ${RBYC_CRUCIBLE}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_CHARGE} starts three containers from your ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE}:"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  ${RBYC_SENTRY}    — runs iptables + dnsmasq, enforces the network allowlist"
  buh_line "  ${RBYC_PENTACLE}  — establishes the network namespace shared with the ${RBYC_BOTTLE}"
  buh_line "  ${RBYC_BOTTLE}    — your workload container (Claude Code lives here)"
  buh_e
  buh_line "The ${RBYC_SENTRY} mediates all traffic. The ${RBYC_BOTTLE} never touches the"
  buh_line "network directly — everything routes through the ${RBYC_SENTRY_P} rules."
  buh_e
  buh_tt  "   " "${RBZ_CRUCIBLE_CHARGE}" "${z_moniker}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "${RBYC_CHARGE} takes 10-30 seconds. It pulls the images from your local"
  buh_line "Docker, creates the ${RBYC_ENCLAVE} network, starts the containers,"
  buh_line "waits for the ${RBYC_SENTRY} to confirm its iptables rules are"
  buh_line "applied, then starts the ${RBYC_BOTTLE}."
  buh_e

  # Charged probe
  if test "${z_crucible_charged}" = "1"; then
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}${RBYC_CRUCIBLE} ${z_moniker} is charged (${RBYC_BOTTLE} container running)"
  else
    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}${RBYC_CRUCIBLE} ${z_moniker} is not charged"
  fi
  buh_e

  # =================================================================
  # Step 4: Enter the container and run Claude Code
  # =================================================================
  buh_step1 "Enter the container and run Claude Code"
  buh_e
  buh_line "SSH into the ${RBYC_BOTTLE}:"
  buh_e
  buh_line "   ${z_cmd_ssh}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "You land as the claude user in ~/workspace, which contains"
  buh_line "a small sample project. Run Claude Code:"
  buh_e

content.text:
Handbook renders cleanly with the new 4-substep structure (1.1 kludge-sentry → 1.2 commit → 1.3 kludge-bottle → 1.4 commit). Fast qualifier passes — tabtarget structure, colophon registrations, and context freshness all clean.

## Current state

**Done:**
- `rbob_kludge_sentry()` added, differential kindle case updated
- `tt/rbw-cKS.KludgeSentry.sh` created and registered
- Zipper entry `RBZ_CRUCIBLE_KLUDGE_SENTRY` enrolled
- First-crucible handbook rewritten: symmetric kludges, commit-between-kludges cadence, manual-paste teaching dropped (option a)
- Tabtarget context regenerated
- Follow-on pace ₢A6AA3 (`handbook-inline-identifier-hygiene`) slated after ₢A6AA2
- Light validation: handbook renders, fast qualifier passes

**Not yet done — task 7 heavy validation** (docket §Validation):
- Full ccyolo kludge cycle: clean tree → `rbw-cKS ccyolo` → commit → `rbw-cKB ccyolo` → commit → `rbw-cC.Charge.ccyolo`
- Dirty-tree guard failure-mode check

This is ~10 min of Docker builds plus two commits that bump `.rbk/ccyolo/rbrn.env` (current hallmarks: sentry k260416, bottle k260417 → today's timestamps).

## Your call

- **(a)** Proceed: I notch pace changes to ₢A6AA2, then run the ccyolo cycle (adds two nameplate-bump commits outside the pace).
- **(b)** You run the cycle yourself and I wait for word.
- **(c)** Defer heavy validation — the handbook and qualifier sign-off is sufficient for now; mark task 7 completed with notes and close the pace.

Which?

message.content: review the pace changes.  Are handbook changes 'surgical' or 'tectonic'?

lastPrompt: review the pace changes.  Are handbook changes 'surgical' or 'tectonic'?

content.text:
**Tectonic — but docketed tectonism, not improvised.**

The plates moved: old Step 2 removed entirely, its content migrated (nameplate definition to Step 1 intro; hallmark probes into their per-substep homes), two new substeps (1.2 and 1.4) inserted, and every step-number below shifted down by one. The "deliberately manual — teaches you how hallmarks live" teaching beat is gone. Section comments had to be hand-renumbered.

Surgical parts: nothing outside the docketed blast radius moved. Charge/Enter/Verify/Quench/Iteration loop untouched. Probes unchanged, just relocated. Yelp trim is clean.

**The move the docket authorized.** Every motion traces to an explicit instruction (collapse old Step 2, insert commit-between-kludges, default to option-a drop-the-manual-paste). Nothing spilled.

**Where to check my judgment:**

1. **Is the manual-paste teaching beat really worth losing?** Docket said default (a) unless mount-time review argues (b). I defaulted. A vestigial "verify your hallmarks are in the nameplate" passive-inspection step (option b) would preserve a teaching moment at the cost of step-numbering churn. Reversible.

2. **Step 1 carrying 4 substeps + shifting downstream numbering vs. promoting the 4 substeps to 4 top-level steps** — I kept it under Step 1 because "get images built with committed hallmarks" reads as one phase. A more tectonic alternative: 9 top-level steps. Less coherent IMO.

3. **Iteration loop at the bottom** — docket said "once-over for commit cadence framing." I left it bottle-only because the sentry-rarely-changes prose is still accurate. Could add one line naming `rbw-cKS` as the parallel when the sentry does change. Judgment: noise > value, skipped.

Want me to sway (1), (2), or (3) before wrap?

These are unnecessary comments, and today they are making our development session more expensive!  So we should plan on removing all of them.  I do want to preserve the sentry kludge op we just added.  Now: do you want to use the substep numbering to make this more legible?  Remember, _we_don't_need_bash_comments_.  The printout is the comment...

content.text:
Agree — trash comments go. My section-dividers like `# ===== / # Step N: / # =====` are parroting what `buh_step1` already renders. They added zero information and cost me (and you) real work when step numbers shifted. BCG violation on my part.

On substep numbering: **no, current structure already does the work.** Step 1 has 4 substeps because "get images built with committed hallmarks" is one coherent phase. Steps 2-5 each do one thing; demoting them under a meta-phase would be artificial hierarchy. `buh_step1`/`buh_step2` IS the self-documenting structure.

**Plan for this pace:** strip just the step-number section-dividers I touched (4 of them). The rest of the trash (`# --- Header ---`, `# --- Probes ---`, `# Docker runtime`, `# Nameplate exists`, etc.) is a broader sweep that predates ₢A6AA2 and deserves its own pace.

Proceeding with strip now.

input.content:
# jjezs_slate handbook-trash-comment-sweep

## Character

Small hygiene pace. Surfaced during ₢A6AA2 when step-number section-divider comments (`# ===== / # Step N: / # =====`) forced hand-renumbering after the step ordering shifted — pure maintenance overhead for zero informational gain. The rendered output (`buh_section`, `buh_step1`, `buh_step2`) IS the self-documenting structure; bash comments that parrot it violate BCG and slow down any future edit that touches step numbering or section ordering.

Cognitive posture: mechanical deletion sweep with a clear taxonomy. Parallel-safe with other A6 work — touches only the rbh0/ handbook family.

## Docket

### Problem

The rbh0/ handbook family carries three classes of bash comments that add no information beyond what the rendered output already says. ₢A6AA2 stripped four instances of class (1) in `rbhofc_first_crucible.sh`; the rest of the family still carries all three classes.

### Three classes (taxonomy)

1. **Section-divider blocks** — `# --- Header ---`, `# --- Probes ---`, `# --- Return to start ---`, `# === / # Step N: / # ===`. The structure they mark is already rendered by `buh_section`, `buh_step1`, `buh_step2`. Pure duplication of rendered output.

2. **Inline "what-pointing" comments** — `# Docker runtime`, `# Nameplate exists`, `# Hallmarks populated in nameplate`, `# Kludge-tagged images exist locally (k-prefixed hallmarks)`, `# Crucible charged`, `# Bottle kludge image probe`, `# Hallmark probes`. Each points at an immediately-following block whose identifiers already say the same thing.

3. **Stale numeric references** — `# Step 3: Charge the crucible` that becomes wrong when an earlier step moves or is removed. Same class as (1) but specifically numerically load-bearing against change — they create maintenance work that should not exist.

### Approach

Sweep `Tools/rbk/rbh0/*.sh` for all three classes and delete. Preserve everything else:

- Copyright/license headers — legally required.
- File-top module docstring (one-line "what is this file") — keep case-by-case only if it adds non-obvious context beyond the filename.
- `# [INFRA-NEEDED]` / `# [ASSUMPTION]` / similar load-bearing markers that track future work or document non-obvious constraints.
- Comments explaining non-obvious **WHY** (hidden constraints, subtle invariants, framework quirks, workaround rationales).

**Discrimination criterion:** does the comment carry non-obvious WHY? If no, strip.

### Implementation steps

1. Inventory: for each `Tools/rbk/rbh0/*.sh`, survey `^\s*#` lines and classify each as (delete / keep / judgment) per the taxonomy.
2. For judgment cases, consult BCG Constant Discipline; err toward delete when the identifier already speaks.
3. Strip. File-by-file, small edits. Run `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` after each file.
4. For each affected handbook, render its tabtarget (`rbw-Occ`, `rbw-Ocr`, `rbw-Ocd`, `rbw-Ofc`, `rbw-Odf`, `rbw-Op`, `rbw-Og`, `rbw-o`, `rbw-h0`) and confirm rendered stdout matches pre-sweep output byte-for-byte.
5. After sweep, final grep across remaining files for new trash that may have crept in.

### Validation

- `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` passes.
- Every affected handbook renders byte-identical stdout vs pre-sweep baseline.

# jjezs_slate handbook-trash-comment-sweep

## Character

Small hygiene pace. Surfaced during ₢A6AA2 when step-number section-divider comments (`# ===== / # Step N: / # =====`) forced hand-renumbering after the step ordering shifted — pure maintenance overhead for zero informational gain. The rendered output (`buh_section`, `buh_step1`, `buh_step2`) IS the self-documenting structure; bash comments that parrot it violate BCG and slow down any future edit that touches step numbering or section ordering.

Cognitive posture: mechanical deletion sweep with a clear taxonomy. Parallel-safe with other A6 work — touches only the rbh0/ handbook family.

## Docket

### Problem

The rbh0/ handbook family carries three classes of bash comments that add no information beyond what the rendered output already says. ₢A6AA2 stripped four instances of class (1) in `rbhofc_first_crucible.sh`; the rest of the family still carries all three classes.

### Three classes (taxonomy)

1. **Section-divider blocks** — `# --- Header ---`, `# --- Probes ---`, `# --- Return to start ---`, `# === / # Step N: / # ===`. The structure they mark is already rendered by `buh_section`, `buh_step1`, `buh_step2`. Pure duplication of rendered output.

2. **Inline "what-pointing" comments** — `# Docker runtime`, `# Nameplate exists`, `# Hallmarks populated in nameplate`, `# Kludge-tagged images exist locally (k-prefixed hallmarks)`, `# Crucible charged`, `# Bottle kludge image probe`, `# Hallmark probes`. Each points at an immediately-following block whose identifiers already say the same thing.

3. **Stale numeric references** — `# Step 3: Charge the crucible` that becomes wrong when an earlier step moves or is removed. Same class as (1) but specifically numerically load-bearing against change — they create maintenance work that should not exist.

### Approach

Sweep `Tools/rbk/rbh0/*.sh` for all three classes and delete. Preserve everything else:

- Copyright/license headers — legally required.
- File-top module docstring (one-line "what is this file") — keep case-by-case only if it adds non-obvious context beyond the filename.
- `# [INFRA-NEEDED]` / `# [ASSUMPTION]` / similar load-bearing markers that track future work or document non-obvious constraints.
- Comments explaining non-obvious **WHY** (hidden constraints, subtle invariants, framework quirks, workaround rationales).

**Discrimination criterion:** does the comment carry non-obvious WHY? If no, strip.

### Implementation steps

1. Inventory: for each `Tools/rbk/rbh0/*.sh`, survey `^\s*#` lines and classify each as (delete / keep / judgment) per the taxonomy.
2. For judgment cases, consult BCG Constant Discipline; err toward delete when the identifier already speaks.
3. Strip. File-by-file, small edits. Run `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` after each file.
4. For each affected handbook, render its tabtarget (`rbw-Occ`, `rbw-Ocr`, `rbw-Ocd`, `rbw-Ofc`, `rbw-Odf`, `rbw-Op`, `rbw-Og`, `rbw-o`, `rbw-h0`) and confirm rendered stdout matches pre-sweep output byte-for-byte.
5. After sweep, final grep across remaining files for new trash that may have crept in.

### Validation

- `tt/rbw-tf.QualifyFast.sh` passes.
- Every affected handbook renders byte-identical stdout vs pre-sweep baseline.
- `git diff` shows pure deletions in bash-comment lines — no code changes, no prose shifts.
- Line count drops measurably. Rough estimate: ~10–40 lines per file × ~20 files = 200–800 lines removed.

### Requires

Nothing. ₢A6AA2 established the pattern (strip trash when touched); this pace generalizes it to the full handbook family.

### Out of scope

- Files outside `Tools/rbk/rbh0/`. Broader BCG-compliance sweep across the rest of `Tools/rbk/` is a later pace or horizon item.
- Any code change — this is comments-only.
- Adding NEW comments.
- Identifier renames (if a comment was compensating for a weak name, a strong rename is the right fix — capture as an itch if encountered, don't inline the rename here).

### Lesson captured

Bash comments that parrot the rendered output are a tax, not an aid. `buh_step1`, `buh_section`, `buh_step2` ARE the structure; `# Step 2: Charge` is cost with no benefit. The principle: the printout is the comment.
